rulings.Case Title: HOMERO ROCHAN ET AL VS. JEFF MOSLEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 25, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANTS HOMERO ROCHAN, DORI ROCHAN AND ROMERO ROCHAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for summary adjudication by Homero Rochan as to the cause of action for slander is granted. Moving party shifted its burden and opposition failed to create a triable issue of fact. Motion is otherwise denied. Multiple triable issues of fact exist regarding slander of title, conspiracy and slander as to Andre. Triable issues of fact include 5, 9, 12, 13, 60, 61. Cross-Complainant to prepare order in accordance with CCP 437c(g). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HOMERO ROCHAN ET AL VS. JEFF MOSLEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 25, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT JEFF MOSLEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Additionally, there are multiple triable issues of fact. See Plaintiff's Response to Defendant's Separate Statement and Defendant's Response to Plaintiff's Additional Material Facts. rulings.Case Title: JASON CHANG VS. ADAM KLEINGBERG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 25, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANTS ADAM KLEINBERG, GLORIA KLEINBERG AND FIRST TEAM PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts supporting a cause of action for wrongful eviction under SFRO 37.9(f), i.e. that landlord served a notice to quit a rental unit, made a demand for possession of a rental unit, or prosecution an Unlawful Detainer action in violation of the Ordinance. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that facts supporting constructive eviction currently alleged in the complaint are sufficient to state a cause of action for wrongful eviction under SFRO 37.9(f). Plaintiff already alleges causes of action for tenant harassment and breach of warranty of habitability and does not support its argument that they may serve as the basis for a cause of action for violation of SFRO 37.9(f) with any authority. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
